title: From One Sentence to a Full Movie Script
description: Show how a single logline like 'a detective who can hear memories' becomes a complete screenplay with beats, scenes, and dialogue in seconds. No writing experience needed.

title: 22 Shot Types Explained in 90 Seconds
description: Demo each shot type back-to-back — Extreme Wide to Rack Focus — showing how selecting a shot type instantly drives the AI generation with correct cinematic framing and emotional tone.

title: Your Phone Is a Motion Capture Studio
description: Film yourself acting a scene with your iPhone or webcam. Watch the performance get extracted and applied to a digital character in Blender — no mocap suit, no studio needed.

title: One Photo Becomes a Digital Actor
act3_feature: Digital Actors from a Single Portrait
description: Upload a single headshot. The AI builds a fully consistent digital character that holds its appearance across every shot in the film — no re-uploading, no drift.

title: The AI Director Crew Working Together
act3_feature: AI Role System (Writer / Director / Cinematographer / Set Designer)
description: Show all five AI roles collaborating on a scene: Writer proposes dialogue, Cinematographer picks angles, Set Designer builds the environment, Director ties it together — human approves each step.

title: Every Shot Gets Its Own Mega Prompt
description: Open a shot in the Mega Prompt Composer and show every layer: narrative, style, camera, lighting, audio, motion — all assembled and editable as YAML before it hits the AI engine.

title: AI Builds Your Set From the Story
act3_feature: Sets Into Story — AI Set Suggestions
description: Hit the 'Sets into Story' button and watch the AI read your script and propose matching virtual environments for each scene. Approve, swap, or customize — sets lock to your locations.

title: Your City Block on Demand
act3_feature: Procedural City on Rails Set Type
description: Define building types — restaurant corner, park, office tower — and watch a full walkable city block generate. Characters move through it with correct walking physics and consistent geography.

title: Plan a Scene Like a Top Director
description: Use the bird's-eye canvas to place cameras, draw movement paths with splines, and visualize field-of-view cones before rendering a single frame. Save credits by planning instead of guessing.

title: Audio Makes Your Character's Face Act
description: Record a voice line. Watch Audio2Face drive brow movement, blinking, emotional changes, and realistic expressions synced frame-by-frame to the audio waveform — far beyond lip-sync.

title: Full Movie Management — From TV Show to Single Shot
act3_feature: Full Production Hierarchy (Show > Season > Episode > Scene > Shot)
description: Scroll through a structured TV series — show level, season, episodes, scenes, shots — all managed in one workspace. Show how changes at the beat level cascade through the whole production.

title: Tell the Story Once — AI Updates Everything
act3_feature: Story Dependency Graph + Automated Calc Engine
description: Edit a story beat and watch the Calc Engine cascade the change through every affected scene, shot, and render prompt automatically. No manual re-work after story decisions.

title: Switch AI Engines Per Shot — Best Quality at Lowest Cost
description: Open the engine picker on three different shots — action sequence, dialogue close-up, landscape wide — and show how each gets routed to the optimal engine with cost shown before confirming.

title: From Script to Finished Video in Two Hours
description: Time-lapse walkthrough: import a 10-page script, approve AI beats, assign sets and actors, generate shots, edit in the timeline, export to 16:9 and 9:16. Traditional pre-production: 80–200 hours.

title: Collaborate Live With Your Whole Team
description: Two people edit the same project simultaneously — live cursors, presence indicators, comment threads, role-based locks. Show a director and writer working on different scenes at the same time.
